q: As the manager hasn’t received all the applications yet, he is not eager to hire anyone. (Use a participle phrase.) [2024]
a: Not having received all the applications yet, the manager is not eager to hire anyone.

q: The thief entered the house. He broke the door lock with a pocket knife. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) [2024]
a: Breaking the door lock with a pocket knife, the thief entered the house.

q: Nilar is a good daughter. She helps her parents at home. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) [2024]
a: Being a good daughter, Nilar helps her parents at home.

q: Mary does not have a KBZPay account. She found it difficult to book an airline ticket online. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) [2024]
a: Not having a KBZ Pay account, Mary found it difficult to book an airline ticket online.

q: At present, people use a GPS device instead of a guidebook when they do not know the way to their destination. (Use ‘a participle phrase’.) [2025]
a: Not knowing the way to their destination, people use a GPS device instead of a guidebook.

q: After he had read the book, he wrote a detailed review. (Use a participle phrase.) [2025]
a: Having read the book, he wrote a detailed review.

q: The housekeeper put on a serious face. She began to tell the story. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) [2025]
a: Putting on a serious face, the housekeeper began to tell the story.

q: As he did not know the way to Ocean Supermarket, he asked a passer-by for help. (Use ‘a participle phrase’.) [2025]
a: Not knowing the way to Ocean Supermarket, he asked a passer-by for help.

q: As he was overwhelmed by the unexpected events, he couldn’t decide how to proceed. (Use a participle phrase.) 2025
a: Overwhelmed by the unexpected events, he couldn’t decide how to proceed.

q: The manager drank his coffee slowly. He thought about the problem. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) 2025
a: Drinking his coffee slowly, the manager thought about the problem.

q: I turned the page of the magazine. I saw her beautiful photograph. (Join, using a participle phrase.) 2025
a: Turning the page of the magazine, I saw her beautiful photograph.

q: Yin Kay held up her phone and started recording. (Join the sentences ‘using a participle phrase.’) 2025
a: Holding up her phone, Yin Kay started recording.

q: After they had completed the project, they celebrated with a team dinner. (Use a participle phrase.) 2025
a: Having completed the project, they celebrated with a team dinner.

q: As he was overwhelmed by the unexpected events, he couldn't decide how to proceed. (Use a participle phrase.) 2025
a: Overwhelmed by the unexpected events, he couldn't decide how to proceed.

q: There was a stormy night with heavy rain. They could not set off on their journey as planned. (Join the sentences using a participle phrase.) [025
a: Being a stormy night with heavy rain, they could not set off on their journey as planned.

q: The chef finished the main vourse. She began to prepare the dessert. (Join the sentences, using a participle phrase.) 2026
a: Finishing/Having finished the main course, the chef began to prepare the dessert.
